role_title: Senior Product Manager, CX & AI Tooling

level_signals: 5+ years of product management experience required

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience with a track record of shipping impactful internal tools, enterprise platforms, or operational tooling where internal users' work directly impacts external customers.

Demonstrated ability to leverage AI/ML capabilities (NLP, automation, intelligent workflows) to solve real user problems, with experience building or integrating AI-powered tooling into operational platforms.
Experience managing a portfolio of products or features simultaneously, making disciplined prioritization tradeoffs in complex, cross-functional environments.

Proven ability to engage meaningfully with engineers on system design, APIs, and data flows, and to communicate product vision and strategy to stakeholders at all levels including executive leadership.
Utilizes generative AI responsibly, maintaining human oversight to deliver business-ready outputs and drive measurable improvements in workflow efficiency, cost, and quality.

scope.surface: CX and AI tooling platform for Coinbase support specialists — intelligent case routing, AI-assisted responses, knowledge retrieval, workflow automation
scope.business_model: internal enterprise platform (B2B2C — internal tooling that impacts external retail customer experience)
core_responsibilities: Own the product roadmap for a portfolio of CX and AI tooling, delivering end-to-end across discovery, requirements, launch, measurement, and iteration
Drive identification and prioritization of the highest-impact problems in the CX space by partnering with frontline specialists
Build AI-integrated features that augment human specialists capabilities, including intelligent case routing, AI-assisted responses, knowledge retrieval, and workflow automation
Define and own success metrics across the portfolio and use data to drive prioritization and measure impact
Execute cross-functional delivery with Design, Data Science, Compliance, and other EAA teams

compensation.equity_form: equity eligibility mentioned alongside bonus and benefits

green_flags: Relocation assistance available for Charlotte, NC
Clear product surface and ownership area with specific metrics named (resolution time, CSAT, specialist efficiency, automation rate)
Remote-first company culture with quarterly in-person surges — not fully office-bound
AI Disclosure section shows transparency about AI use in hiring process
red_flags: Base salary range is a single point ($180,370–$180,370) — no range, which is unusual and limits negotiation transparency
Role requires relocation to Greater Charlotte, NC area — eliminates fully remote candidates despite company being 'remote-first'
No nice-to-haves listed — unusually sparse JD may reflect incomplete requirements or high bar with little flexibility
Application limit: maximum 3 applications within a 6-month period noted

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior Product Manager' and 5+ years requirement)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no direct reports mentioned)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description as internal platform impacting external customers)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (directly quoted from JD responsibilities section; labeled inferred because they are listed as metrics to define, not company KPIs)
location.type (JD says 'remote-first but not remote-only' and lists specific Charlotte, NC location with relocation assistance — treated as hybrid/onsite-required)
ambiguities: Compensation range shows $180,370–$180,370 (identical min and max) — may be a single pay band point or a data entry error; treated as stated but flagged as unusual

Location: JD describes Coinbase as 'remote-first, but not remote-only' but then lists 'Greater Charlotte, NC area' with relocation assistance, implying on-site or hybrid presence is expected. Treated as hybrid with onsite expectation.
Visa sponsorship not mentioned anywhere in the JD — marked as 'unclear'
